metadata.title: grown-workspace - Component Definition
metadata.remarks: Hand-authored against the OSCAL 1.1.x JSON schema; UNVALIDATED by an automated OSCAL validator. UUIDs are fixed placeholders. control-id tokens are NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 (lowercase, e.g. 'ac-2', 'au-2.3'). 800-171 mappings are carried as props (name='control-171') alongside their 800-53 owner where applicable. implementation-status values: implemented | partial | planned | not-applicable. grown is NOT authorized; see compliance/README.md.

components.title: Auth / Zitadel OIDC Integration
components.description: grown's authentication layer (internal/auth). Delegates all identity to a Zitadel OIDC provider: Login mints a CSRF state cookie and redirects to the IdP; Callback validates state, exchanges the code, upserts the user (internal/users), and creates an opaque server-side session (internal/auth/session.go) delivered as an HttpOnly/Secure/host-only cookie (internal/auth/config.go, service.go). Sessions are revocable and time-bound. The in-app account-security panel (internal/zitadelproxy + web components/security/*) proxies a narrow slice of the Zitadel User API v2 so users self-manage TOTP, passkeys (FIDO2/WebAuthn), and password without leaving grown.

control-implementations.description: Controls satisfied by grown's Auth/Zitadel integration. Primary owner of the IA family; shared owner of AC. CMMC L2 / 800-171 equivalents carried in props.

implemented-requirements.description: Every grown request is authenticated against a Zitadel-issued session before any service mutates state. auth.HTTPMiddleware (internal/auth/middleware.go) looks up the session cookie, resolves the user, and attaches it to context; service methods reject calls with no UserFromContext (e.g. AuthService.Whoami returns Unauthenticated).

MFA is CAPABLE but not enforced. The Zitadel proxy (internal/zitadelproxy/proxy.go) exposes TOTP and passkey/FIDO2 registration via the Zitadel User API v2, and the web security panel (components/security/*) drives it. Org-wide MFA enforcement is a Zitadel login-policy setting that is not yet turned on — see POA&M risk poam-mfa.

Identifiers are managed by Zitadel (the OIDC 'sub' claim) and mirrored into grown.users (oidc_issuer, oidc_subject) with a UNIQUE (org_id, oidc_issuer, oidc_subject) constraint (migration 0002). users.UpsertByOIDC keys on issuer+subject so a user maps to exactly one immutable identity.

Authenticator management is delegated to Zitadel: password policy, TOTP enrollment, passkey/FIDO2 registration, and password change are all driven through the User API v2 proxy. The proxy authorizes that {id} in /v2/users/{id}/... equals the caller's own oidc_subject (proxy.go authorizeUserAccess), so a user can only manage their own authenticators.

Password complexity, hashing (bcrypt/Argon2), history, and lockout are enforced by Zitadel, not grown. grown never stores passwords; it holds only opaque session tokens (internal/auth/session.go). Inherited from the Zitadel component; grown's responsibility is to never bypass it (it does not).

Hardware authenticator (FIDO2/WebAuthn) support: the proxy injects the request Host as the WebAuthn RP id on passkey-registration starts (proxy.go injectPasskeyDomain), binding credentials to the origin the user is actually on. Backed by Zitadel WebAuthn.
All identity is federated to an external OIDC IdP (Zitadel). grown is purely a relying party; it issues no local passwords.
Unsuccessful-logon lockout is enforced upstream by Zitadel (failed-attempt lockout policy). grown surfaces only generic auth errors and never reveals account state.

Session termination: sessions are opaque tokens persisted in grown.sessions with an expires_at and nullable revoked_at. SessionStore.Lookup returns ErrSessionExpired/ErrSessionRevoked (session.go); Logout calls Revoke and clears the cookie (service.go). Expired/revoked sessions are rejected on the next request.

Access enforcement: every authenticated request carries a resolved user AND org in context (auth.WithUser/WithOrg). Privileged operations (admin settings, audit viewer, adminusers) additionally gate on the GROWN_ADMIN_EMAILS allowlist (internal/admin/service.go requireAdmin, internal/audit/handler.go). NOTE: grown has no role/RBAC model; admin authority is an env allowlist with an open fallback when empty — see POA&M poam-rbac.

components.title: Audit Subsystem
components.description: Cross-cutting activity trail (internal/audit). A gRPC unary interceptor (interceptor.go) records every MUTATING RPC (Create/Update/Delete/Share/Move/...) across ALL services after the handler runs; an HTTP middleware (.Log(), middleware.go) wraps every binary upload/download/stream route (drive, mail/chat attachments, photos, books, video, music — wired in internal/server/server.go). Events land in the append-only grown.audit_events table (migration 0037) with org_id, actor_id/email, service, action, resource_type/id, full method, status, JSONB detail, client IP (X-Forwarded-For first hop), user agent, and created_at. An admin-gated viewer (handler.go, GET /api/v1/admin/audit, web app/admin) lists an org's events with service/actor/action/limit/before filters.

control-implementations.description: Controls satisfied by grown's audit subsystem. Primary owner of the AU family (event capture + content + protection + review).

implemented-requirements.description: Auditable events: every state-changing action is captured automatically. The interceptor's mutatingPrefixes list (Create, Update, Delete, Trash, Set, Submit, Post, Add, Remove, Move, Copy, Share, Rename, Reorder, Upsert, Generate) selects RPCs; read-only RPCs (List/Get/Search/Whoami/Health) are intentionally skipped. Binary routes are explicitly wrapped with action labels (upload/download/stream/attachment-*). One interceptor registration auto-covers every current and future gRPC service.

Audit record content: each grown.audit_events row records timestamp (created_at), event type (service+action+method), outcome (status ok|error plus gRPC code/HTTP status in detail), actor identity (actor_id, actor_email), source (ip, user_agent), and affected object (resource_type, resource_id). See repository.go Event struct and migration 0037.
Additional audit content: the JSONB detail column carries the gRPC code (on error) and HTTP status, and the method column carries the full gRPC method path or 'VERB path' for cross-system tracing.

Audit review: org admins review events through GET /api/v1/admin/audit (handler.go), filtered by service/actor/action and keyset-paginated by 'before'. Authorization requires an authenticated caller whose email is in GROWN_ADMIN_EMAILS. Review is manual / on-demand; there is no automated alerting (see POA&M poam-siem).

Protection of audit information: audit_events is append-only by convention (the repository exposes only Insert + List — no Update/Delete code path). Read access via the viewer is admin-gated. NOTE: append-only is enforced only by the absence of mutating code, NOT by a database-level constraint or revoked DELETE grant — see POA&M poam-au9.

Audit generation: a single audit-generation capability (the interceptor + HTTP middleware) is centrally wired in internal/server/server.go and covers every service uniformly, so coverage cannot drift per-service. Generation is best-effort and never blocks the user's request (recorder.go).

Audit storage capacity: events persist to Postgres with org/created_at and org/service indexes. There is NO retention, rotation, off-host forwarding, or capacity-alerting policy — see POA&M poam-retention.

components.title: API Gateway (gRPC + grpc-gateway REST)
components.description: The single server binary (internal/server, cmd/server) exposes gRPC on :9000 and a grpc-gateway REST surface on :8080 that also serves the built React SPA. It wires the auth HTTP middleware (resolving session->user->org into context), the audit interceptor, the admin allowlist, the Zitadel proxy mount, and per-org service enablement (internal/admin). It is the policy-enforcement point where authentication, tenancy resolution, and audit capture converge.

control-implementations.description: Controls satisfied at the API gateway / policy-enforcement point. Shared owner of AC and AU.

implemented-requirements.description: Information flow enforcement: the Zitadel proxy restricts forwarded paths to /v2/users/{id}/... and forbids accessing another user's id (proxy.go authorizeUserAccess); it strips the caller's grown Cookie before forwarding so the session never leaks upstream, and replaces Authorization with the service PAT (copyProxyHeaders). Body size is bounded (1 MiB LimitReader).

Least privilege: privileged surfaces (SetServiceSettings, /api/v1/admin/audit, adminusers) require membership in GROWN_ADMIN_EMAILS; read-only admin reads are allowed to any authenticated member. The allowlist is an interim mechanism pending a real role model (see POA&M poam-rbac), and falls open when empty.

Denial-of-service protection (partial): the Zitadel proxy caps request bodies and sets upstream timeouts (DefaultTimeout 30s). There is no rate limiting, connection throttling, or WAF in front of the gateway beyond the Cloudflare/cluster edge (see POA&M poam-boundary).

Permitted actions without identification: only OIDC discovery and the login/callback handshake are reachable unauthenticated; all data-bearing endpoints require a resolved user (UserFromContext) or return Unauthenticated.

components.title: PostgreSQL (per-tenant data store)
components.description: Single Postgres instance holding all grown data in the 'grown' schema (internal/storage, migrations 0001-0038). Every domain table carries org_id (migration 0002 onward); repositories scope all queries by org_id resolved from context. Stores users, opaque sessions, audit_events, and per-app data. In the gitops deployment Postgres runs under CloudNativePG on the Talos cluster.

control-implementations.description: Controls satisfied by the Postgres data layer. Shared owner of AC (tenant isolation), SC (data protection), AU (audit storage), CP (backup).

implemented-requirements.description: Access enforcement / multi-tenant isolation: every table has an org_id FK to grown.orgs; every repository query filters by the caller's org_id (e.g. audit Repository.List rejects an empty org and scopes WHERE org_id = $1). A user resolves to exactly one org via UNIQUE (org_id, oidc_issuer, oidc_subject).

Protection of information at rest: encryption-at-rest depends entirely on the underlying disk (e.g. cloud-provider volume encryption). grown does NOT implement application-level encryption-at-rest for Postgres data, and there is no attestation that the deployed volume is encrypted — see POA&M poam-rest. SOPS+age protects deployment SECRETS, not row data.

Audit record retention: audit_events persist in Postgres indefinitely (no purge job), which over-retains rather than under-retains. There is no defined retention period, archival, or off-host copy — see POA&M poam-retention.

System backup: in the gitops deployment, CloudNativePG provides Postgres backups and Velero backs up cluster state. This is an INHERITED operational control from pick-gitops, not implemented in grown's code, and is unverified here — see POA&M poam-contingency.

components.title: Blob Store (RustFS / S3-compatible)
components.description: Object storage for user-uploaded binaries (drive files, mail/chat attachments, photos, books, video, music). grown talks to it via an S3 client (internal/drive/blobs.go) pointed at a RustFS endpoint with static access/secret keys. Upload/download/stream routes are all wrapped by the audit middleware. The PDF/pdf app (pdf/) additionally encrypts signing private keys with an AES-256-GCM KEK before storage (pdf/backend/internal/crypto/keystore.go).

control-implementations.description: Controls touching the blob store. Shared owner of SC (data protection) and AU (access audit).

implemented-requirements.description: Cryptographic protection: the PDF app encrypts stored signing private keys with AES-256-GCM using a KEK (keystore.go). This is correct AEAD but uses the Go standard-library crypto, which is NOT a FIPS 140-validated module. No FIPS-validated cryptography is in use anywhere in grown — see POA&M poam-fips.

Protection at rest: blob payloads are NOT encrypted at the application layer (the drive Blobs wrapper does no SSE configuration). At-rest protection depends on the RustFS backing volume's disk encryption, which is unattested here. The PDF KEK is the sole app-level at-rest encryption and is scoped to signing keys only.

Blob access auditing: every upload/download/stream route is wrapped with auditRec.Log(service, action, ...) in internal/server/server.go, so object access is captured in audit_events with the resource id sniffed from the path.

components.title: Gateway / TLS Termination (cert-manager + Gateway API)
components.description: In the gitops deployment, TLS is terminated at the Talos/Kubernetes edge via the Gateway API, with cert-manager issuing a Let's Encrypt wildcard certificate (ACME). grown's own cookies are flagged Secure (CookieSecure) on the assumption that TLS terminates in front of it; the app binary itself serves plaintext HTTP/gRPC behind the gateway and does NOT terminate TLS (no ListenAndServeTLS in cmd/server or internal/server).

control-implementations.description: Controls satisfied at the TLS/edge boundary. Primary owner of SC transport-protection controls; inherited from the pick-gitops cert-manager component.

implemented-requirements.description: Transmission confidentiality/integrity: external traffic reaches grown over HTTPS terminated at the Gateway. grown sets Secure on session/state cookies (config.go CookieSecure) so they are only sent over TLS. INHERITED from cert-manager; intra-cluster hop from gateway to the grown pod may be plaintext unless a mesh is enforced (unverified) — see POA&M poam-boundary.

Cryptographic key establishment & management: certificate lifecycle (issuance, renewal, key generation) is automated by cert-manager via ACME/Let's Encrypt. INHERITED operational control from pick-gitops; not implemented in grown.

Session authenticity: session cookies are HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=Lax with an opaque 256-bit random token (session.go newToken). OIDC login is CSRF-protected by a short-lived state cookie validated on callback (service.go).
Cryptographic protection (transport): TLS uses Let's Encrypt-issued certificates and the cluster's TLS stack, which is NOT FIPS 140-validated. See POA&M poam-fips.

components.title: GitOps / Flux Deployment & Config Management
components.description: grown is built as a single container image (Dockerfile: multi-stage, non-root user uid 10001, CGO disabled, embedded migrations) and deployed declaratively. In the broader platform, Flux reconciles cluster state from git and SOPS+age encrypts secrets at rest in the repo. Database schema is versioned as ordered SQL migrations embedded in the binary (internal/storage/migrate.go).

control-implementations.description: Controls satisfied by the build/deploy/config-management pipeline. Owner of CM and parts of SA; mostly inherited from pick-gitops.

implemented-requirements.description: Baseline configuration: the deployed state is fully declared in git (manifests + the pinned container image) and reconciled by Flux, giving a version-controlled, diff-able baseline. Schema baseline is the ordered migration set.

Configuration change control: changes flow through git commits/PRs and Flux reconciliation, providing a change record and a single promotion path. There is no formal change-approval board or documented CCB process — partial.

Configuration settings: app config is supplied via environment variables validated at startup (auth.Config.Validate rejects missing IssuerURL/ClientID/secret/etc.), failing closed on misconfiguration.
Least functionality: the runtime image is minimal (alpine + only ca-certificates, pandoc for Docs export, tzdata), runs as a non-root user (uid 10001), and exposes only :8080/:9000.

No embedded unencrypted static authenticators: secrets (OIDC client secret, DB creds, Zitadel PAT, blob keys, PDF KEK) are injected at runtime via env/secret refs; deployment secrets are SOPS+age encrypted in git. No credentials are committed in plaintext.
Development process: reproducible builds (pinned Go/Node/buf versions, trimpath, CGO disabled). No SAST/dependency-scan/SBOM gate is enforced in the grown pipeline itself — see POA&M poam-vulnscan.
